audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Jacob's departure, Laban's changing wages, dream revelation, household gods, Rachel's concealment, covenant heap, Mizpah/Galeed naming, and oath boundaries.

verses.restored: Jacob heard the words of Laban’s sons, saying, Jacob has taken all that was our father’s, and from what was our father’s he has made all this weight/wealth.

familiar.text: And he heard the words of Laban's sons, saying, Jacob hath taken away all that was our father's...

verses.restored: Jacob saw the face of Laban, and behold, it was not toward him as before.

familiar.text: And Jacob beheld the countenance of Laban...

verses.restored: The Eternal said to Jacob, Return to the land of your fathers and to your kindred, and I will be with you.

familiar.text: And the LORD said unto Jacob, Return unto the land of thy fathers...

notes.title: Return command
notes.body: The Bethel promise of return now becomes a command.

verses.restored: Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah to the field, to his flock.

familiar.text: And Jacob sent and called Rachel and Leah to the field unto his flock,

verses.restored: He said to them, I see your father’s face, that it is not toward me as before, but the Divine of my father has been with me.

familiar.text: And said unto them, I see your father's countenance...

verses.restored: You know that with all my strength I have served your father.

familiar.text: And ye know that with all my power I have served your father.

verses.restored: Yet your father has deceived me and changed my wages ten times, but the Divine did not allow him to do harm to me.

familiar.text: And your father hath deceived me, and changed my wages ten times...

verses.restored: If he said, The speckled shall be your wages, then all the flock bore speckled; and if he said, The striped shall be your wages, then all the flock bore striped.

familiar.text: If he said thus, The speckled shall be thy wages...

verses.restored: So the Divine has taken away the livestock of your father and given them to me.

familiar.text: Thus God hath taken away the cattle of your father, and given them to me.

verses.restored: At the time the flock was breeding, I lifted my eyes and saw in a dream: behold, the male goats mounting the flock were striped, speckled, and mottled.

familiar.text: And it came to pass at the time that the cattle conceived...

verses.restored: The messenger of the Divine said to me in the dream, Jacob. I said, Here I am.

familiar.text: And the angel of God spake unto me in a dream, saying, Jacob...

verses.restored: He said, Lift your eyes now and see: all the male goats mounting the flock are striped, speckled, and mottled, for I have seen all that Laban is doing to you.

familiar.text: And he said, Lift up now thine eyes, and see...

verses.restored: I am the Divine of Bethel, where you anointed a pillar and where you vowed a vow to me. Now rise, go out from this land, and return to the land of your kindred.

familiar.text: I am the God of Bethel, where thou anointedst the pillar...

verses.restored: Rachel and Leah answered and said to him, Is there still any portion or inheritance for us in our father’s house?

familiar.text: And Rachel and Leah answered and said unto him, Is there yet any portion...

verses.restored: Are we not counted by him as foreigners? For he has sold us, and has completely eaten up our silver.

familiar.text: Are we not counted of him strangers? for he hath sold us...

notes.title: Sold us
notes.body: Rachel and Leah interpret Laban’s treatment as exploitation rather than fatherly provision.

verses.restored: All the wealth that the Divine has taken from our father belongs to us and to our children. Now then, all that the Divine has said to you, do.

familiar.text: For all the riches which God hath taken from our father...

verses.restored: Jacob rose and lifted his children and his women onto the camels.

familiar.text: Then Jacob rose up, and set his sons and his wives upon camels;

verses.restored: He drove all his livestock and all his possessions that he had gathered, the livestock of his acquisition that he had gathered in Paddan-Aram, to go to Isaac his father in the land of Canaan.

familiar.text: And he carried away all his cattle, and all his goods...

verses.restored: Laban had gone to shear his sheep, and Rachel stole the household gods that belonged to her father.

familiar.text: And Laban went to shear his sheep: and Rachel had stolen the images...

notes.title: Household gods
notes.body: Teraphim are household cult objects; Rachel’s motive is not stated.

verses.restored: Jacob stole the heart of Laban the Aramean, by not telling him that he was fleeing.

familiar.text: And Jacob stole away unawares to Laban the Syrian...

notes.title: Stole the heart
notes.body: A Hebrew idiom meaning deceived or acted without informing.

verses.restored: He fled with all that was his, rose, crossed the River, and set his face toward the hill country of Gilead.

familiar.text: So he fled with all that he had; and he rose up, and passed over the river...

verses.restored: On the third day, Laban was told that Jacob had fled.

familiar.text: And it was told Laban on the third day that Jacob was fled.

verses.restored: He took his brothers with him and pursued him seven days’ journey, and overtook him in the hill country of Gilead.

familiar.text: And he took his brethren with him, and pursued after him seven days' journey...

verses.restored: The Divine came to Laban the Aramean in a dream by night and said to him, Guard yourself, lest you speak with Jacob from good to bad.

familiar.text: And God came to Laban the Syrian in a dream by night...

notes.title: From good to bad
notes.body: An idiom warning Laban not to threaten or manipulate Jacob.

verses.restored: Laban overtook Jacob. Jacob had pitched his tent in the hill country, and Laban with his brothers pitched in the hill country of Gilead.

familiar.text: Then Laban overtook Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent...

verses.restored: Laban said to Jacob, What have you done? You stole my heart and drove my daughters away like captives of the sword.

familiar.text: And Laban said to Jacob, What hast thou done...

verses.restored: Why did you flee secretly and steal me, and not tell me, so that I might have sent you away with joy and songs, with tambourine and lyre?

familiar.text: Wherefore didst thou flee away secretly, and steal away from me...

verses.restored: You did not allow me to kiss my sons and my daughters. Now you have acted foolishly.

familiar.text: And hast not suffered me to kiss my sons and my daughters?...

verses.restored: It is in the power of my hand to do harm to you, but the Divine of your father spoke to me last night, saying, Guard yourself from speaking with Jacob from good to bad.

familiar.text: It is in the power of my hand to do you hurt...

verses.restored: Now you have surely gone because you deeply longed for your father’s house. But why did you steal my gods?

familiar.text: And now, though thou wouldest needs be gone... wherefore hast thou stolen my gods?

verses.restored: Jacob answered and said to Laban, Because I was afraid, for I said, Lest you take your daughters from me by force.

familiar.text: And Jacob answered and said to Laban, Because I was afraid...

verses.restored: With whomever you find your gods, he shall not live. Before our brothers, recognize what is yours with me and take it. Jacob did not know that Rachel had stolen them.

familiar.text: With whomsoever thou findest thy gods, let him not live...

notes.title: Dangerous oath
notes.body: Jacob unknowingly places Rachel in danger with his words.

verses.restored: Laban went into Jacob’s tent, Leah’s tent, and the tent of the two slave-women, but he did not find them. He went out from Leah’s tent and entered Rachel’s tent.

familiar.text: And Laban went into Jacob's tent, and into Leah's tent...

verses.restored: Rachel had taken the household gods and put them in the camel’s saddle, and sat on them. Laban felt through all the tent but did not find them.

familiar.text: Now Rachel had taken the images, and put them in the camel's furniture...

verses.restored: She said to her father, Let there be no anger in my lord’s eyes that I cannot rise before you, for the way of women is upon me. He searched but did not find the household gods.

familiar.text: And she said to her father, Let it not displease my lord that I cannot rise up...

verses.restored: Jacob became angry and argued with Laban. Jacob answered and said to Laban, What is my transgression? What is my offense, that you have hotly pursued me?

familiar.text: And Jacob was wroth, and chode with Laban...

verses.restored: Though you have felt through all my goods, what have you found from all the goods of your house? Set it here before my brothers and your brothers, and let them decide between us two.

familiar.text: Whereas thou hast searched all my stuff, what hast thou found...

verses.restored: These twenty years I have been with you; your ewes and female goats have not miscarried, and the rams of your flock I have not eaten.

familiar.text: This twenty years have I been with thee; thy ewes and thy she goats...

verses.restored: What was torn by beasts I did not bring to you; I bore the loss myself. From my hand you required it, whether stolen by day or stolen by night.

familiar.text: That which was torn of beasts I brought not unto thee...

verses.restored: By day heat consumed me, and cold by night, and my sleep fled from my eyes.

familiar.text: Thus I was; in the day the drought consumed me, and the frost by night...

verses.restored: These twenty years I have been in your house. I served you fourteen years for your two daughters and six years for your flock, and you changed my wages ten times.

familiar.text: Thus have I been twenty years in thy house; I served thee fourteen years...

verses.restored: If the Divine of my father, the Divine of Abraham and the Fear of Isaac, had not been with me, surely now you would have sent me away empty. The Divine saw my affliction and the toil of my hands, and rebuked you last night.

familiar.text: Except the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the fear of Isaac...

notes.title: Fear of Isaac
notes.body: A striking divine title tied to Isaac’s reverent awe or the God whom Isaac fears.

verses.restored: Laban answered and said to Jacob, The daughters are my daughters, the children are my children, the flock is my flock, and all that you see is mine. But what can I do today to these my daughters or to their children whom they have borne?

familiar.text: And Laban answered and said unto Jacob, These daughters are my daughters...

verses.restored: Now come, let us cut a covenant, I and you, and let it be a witness between me and you.

familiar.text: Now therefore come thou, let us make a covenant, I and thou...

verses.restored: Jacob took a stone and set it up as a pillar.

familiar.text: And Jacob took a stone, and set it up for a pillar.

verses.restored: Jacob said to his brothers, Gather stones. They took stones and made a heap, and they ate there by the heap.

familiar.text: And Jacob said unto his brethren, Gather stones...

verses.restored: Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha, but Jacob called it Galeed.

familiar.text: And Laban called it Jegarsahadutha: but Jacob called it Galeed.

notes.title: Aramaic/Hebrew naming
notes.body: Both names mean heap of witness, one in Aramaic, one in Hebrew.

verses.restored: Laban said, This heap is a witness between me and you today. Therefore its name was called Galeed,

familiar.text: And Laban said, This heap is a witness between me and thee this day...

verses.restored: and Mizpah, for he said, May the Eternal watch between me and you when we are hidden from each other.

familiar.text: And Mizpah; for he said, The LORD watch between me and thee...

notes.title: Mizpah
notes.body: Often quoted sentimentally, but here it functions as a boundary-watch between mistrustful parties.

verses.restored: If you afflict my daughters, or if you take women besides my daughters, though no one is with us, see, the Divine is witness between me and you.

familiar.text: If thou shalt afflict my daughters, or if thou shalt take other wives beside my daughters...

verses.restored: Laban said to Jacob, Look at this heap, and look at the pillar that I have set between me and you.

familiar.text: And Laban said to Jacob, Behold this heap, and behold this pillar...

verses.restored: This heap is witness, and the pillar is witness, that I will not pass over this heap to you, and you will not pass over this heap and this pillar to me, for harm.

familiar.text: This heap be witness, and this pillar be witness...

verses.restored: May the Divine of Abraham and the divine of Nahor judge between us, the divine of their father. Jacob swore by the Fear of his father Isaac.

familiar.text: The God of Abraham, and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge betwixt us...

notes.title: Different divine references
notes.body: The verse preserves the complexity of ancestral divine language; Jacob swears by the Fear of Isaac.

verses.restored: Jacob offered a sacrifice on the mountain and called his brothers to eat bread. They ate bread and spent the night on the mountain.

familiar.text: Then Jacob offered sacrifice upon the mount, and called his brethren to eat bread...

verses.restored: Early in the morning Laban rose, kissed his sons and daughters, and blessed them. Then Laban went and returned to his place.

familiar.text: And early in the morning Laban rose up, and kissed his sons and his daughters...
